challenges.title: Define and Use Custom Properties
challenges.description: Write CSS that defines custom properties (CSS variables) in `:root` and uses them with `var()`. In the `:root` selector, define `--primary-color` as `#3498db`, `--font-size` as `16px`, and `--spacing` as `1rem`. Create a rule for `.card` that uses `color: var(--primary-color)`, `font-size: var(--font-size)`, and `padding: var(--spacing)`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert ':root' in css.lower(), 'Should define variables in :root'
assert '--primary-color' in css.lower(), 'Should define --primary-color variable'
assert '--font-size' in css.lower(), 'Should define --font-size variable'
assert '--spacing' in css.lower(), 'Should define --spacing variable'
assert 'var(' in css.lower(), 'Should use var() to reference variables'
assert '.card' in css.lower(), 'Should target .card'
hints: Custom properties always start with two dashes: --property-name
Define them in :root to make them globally available, then use var(--name) to reference them

challenges.title: Fallback Values and Scoped Variables
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses `var()` with fallback values and component-scoped custom properties. In `:root`, define `--gap` as `20px`. Create a rule for `.button` that defines its own scoped `--btn-bg` as `#2ecc71` and uses `background: var(--btn-bg)`, `padding: var(--btn-padding, 10px 20px)` (with a fallback since `--btn-padding` is not defined), and `margin: var(--gap)`. Create a rule for `.button.danger` that overrides `--btn-bg` to `#e74c3c`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert ':root' in css.lower(), 'Should define global variables in :root'
assert 'var(' in css.lower(), 'Should use var() function'
assert '--btn-bg' in css.lower(), 'Should define component-scoped --btn-bg variable'
assert '--btn-padding' in css.lower(), 'Should reference --btn-padding with a fallback'
assert '.button' in css.lower(), 'Should target .button'
assert 'danger' in css.lower(), 'Should target .button.danger to override variable'
assert '--gap' in css.lower(), 'Should define --gap variable'
hints: var(--name, fallback) uses the fallback value when the variable is not defined
Redefining a custom property in a more specific selector overrides it for that scope
